title: Endpoint Analysis
purpose: Score repair endpoint gels, classify colony phenotypes, and export structured endpoint assay data.
learningTime: Learn this tool in under 3 minutes

tour.title: Experiment metadata
tour.body: Enter strain name and colony count to contextualize your endpoint assay.

tour.title: Workflow tabs
tour.body: Move between gel scoring, colony classification, data tables, and charts.

tour.title: Score gels
tour.body: Upload endpoint gels and assign lane scores according to your assay protocol.

tour.title: Export
tour.body: Download Excel with scored lanes, classifications, and summary statistics.

overview.purpose: Support Bloom Lab endpoint repair assays with integrated gel scoring and colony phenotype tracking.
overview.useCase: Document endpoint formation frequency and colony morphology classes for strain comparisons.
overview.inputs: Gel images, lane scores, colony classifications.
overview.outputs: Summary tables, charts, and Excel exports.

workflow.title: Set metadata
workflow.description: Enter strain and assay parameters in the header.

workflow.title: Score gels
workflow.description: Upload gels and record per-lane endpoint scores.

workflow.title: Classify colonies
workflow.description: Mark colonies and assign phenotype categories.

tips: Score gels consistently using the same brightness reference across the session.
Use colony categories defined in your protocol document.
Autosave preserves work if you close the tab accidentally.

faq.a: Yes. Autosave stores progress locally and offers restore on return.

items: Integrated gel scoring and colony classification
